\begin{minipage}{0.95\linewidth}
\footnotesize
\textit{Note.} Rating model: $T_i\sim\mathrm{Categorical}(p)$; conditional on $T_i$, rater $j$ reports $T_i$ with probability $\rho_{ij}$ and otherwise guesses from $p$. Missingness indicators $M_{ij}\in\{0,1\}$ are independent across $(i,j)$ given the specified mechanism. DGP~A: exchangeable raters ($\rho_{ij}=0.92$) and MCAR with rater-specific $\pi_j=\Pr(M_{ij}=1)$ given by $(0.95, 0.90, 0.80, 0.65, 0.50, 0.35)$. DGP~B: non-exchangeable raters with $\rho_{ij}=\rho_j$ where $\rho_j=(0.98, 0.96, 0.92, 0.85, 0.72, 0.60)$ and MCAR with $\pi_j=(0.95, 0.85, 0.70, 0.45, 0.25, 0.15)$ (higher-skill raters observed more often). DGP~C: category-dependent difficulty with $\rho_{ij}=0.92\,m(T_i)$ where $m=(1.05, 1.00, 0.95, 0.85, 0.70)$, and MAR with $\pi_c=\Pr(M_{ij}=1\mid T_i=c)=(0.95, 0.90, 0.80, 0.55, 0.25)$. Main table uses nominal (identity) loss; Appendix table uses linear loss $\ell(a,b)=|a-b|/(C-1)$ (treating categories as ordered).
\end{minipage}
\end{table}
